llm_training.natural_language_description: This product listing refers to a set of 50 pairs of individually wrapped disposable chopsticks sold via field survey data by The Noodle Place (Circular Road), marketed specifically for use during Chinese New Year Yu Sheng (Lo Hei) celebrations. Yu Sheng, also known as Yusheng or Lo Hei, is a Singaporean-Chinese festive raw fish salad tossed communally during the Lunar New Year for good luck. Disposable chopsticks of this type are typically made from bamboo or wood, hygienically sealed in individual paper or plastic wrappers, and are intended as single-use eating utensils. They are not a food item and contribute no calories, macronutrients, or micronutrients to the diet. From a nutritional and clinical standpoint, they are entirely inert. The product is relevant to the food delivery context as a tableware accessory bundled with festive food orders. All dietary flags (vegetarian, vegan, halal, kosher, gluten-free, dairy-free) are applicable as the item is a non-food utensil. No allergen concerns are associated with standard bamboo or wood chopsticks, though individuals with wood sensitivities should be aware of the material composition.
common_user_queries: Where can I buy disposable chopsticks for Yu Sheng?
Chopsticks for Lo Hei delivery Singapore
Bamboo chopsticks for Chinese New Year
meal_context: Yu Sheng / Lo Hei tossing ceremony

confidence_reason: This item is unambiguously a non-food utensil (disposable chopsticks) sold via a food delivery platform. The determination that it contributes zero nutrients is self-evident and requires no nutritional database lookup. All zero macro values are justified by the non-food nature of the product. Confidence is high for the classification and nutritional determination.
notes: This record describes a non-food utensil product — 50 pairs of individually wrapped disposable chopsticks — sold via field survey data for use during Chinese New Year Yu Sheng celebrations. As a non-food item, it contributes no nutrients to the diet. Zero-macro justification: energy_kcal is naturally zero as this is a non-food utensil that is not consumed. protein_g is naturally zero as chopsticks contain no protein. fat_g is naturally zero as chopsticks contain no fat. carbohydrate_g is naturally zero as chopsticks contain no carbohydrates. sugar_g is naturally zero. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero. sodium_mg is naturally zero. All micronutrient fields (potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g) are set to -1 as they are not applicable or not reported for a non-food utensil item; no nutritional database reports these values for chopsticks. glycemic_index is set to 1 (effectively zero) as this is a non-food item containing no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category is low per Veritas convention for non-glycemic items. No ontology codes are assigned as FoodOn, FoodEx2, and SNOMED CT do not classify non-food utensils. The food_group is retained as Mixed from the input as no more specific group applies to a non-food tableware item. Multilingual names are provided per Singaporean cuisine rules (English, Chinese simplified, Chinese traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as the product is sold in Singapore in a Singaporean cultural context.
